response.justification: While the candidate is an accomplished engineer with 20 years of professional experience and currently a Staff Engineer at Google, they lack the mandatory technical qualifications for this role. C++ development experience is minimal (~1 year in 2014-2015) rather than the required 5-10 years, and is not even listed in their current skills. Windows platform expertise is absent from their background. Most critically, the candidate has zero experience in financial services, capital markets, or fixed income domains, and their recent 10-year career trajectory has moved entirely away from systems-level C++ programming into web, mobile, and general backend development.
strengths: Extensive software engineering experience (20 years) currently at Google Staff level, demonstrating high technical caliber and growth trajectory
Excellent verbal and written communication demonstrated through public talks, workshops, and technical education programs
Proven ability to master diverse technology stacks and work across multiple domains, showing adaptability and learning capacity
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~1 year at National Instruments in 2014-2015) and absent from current skills, far below the mandatory 5-10 year requirement
No evidence of Windows platform development; entire recent career (10+ years) is in web/mobile stacks with Node.js, TypeScript, JavaScript, and D language
Complete absence of experience in financial services, capital markets, investment banking, or fixed income—mandatory domain requirement with no demonstrated pathway to acquire this expertise
